# coJournalist — Full documentation

> AI-powered local news monitoring for journalists and newsrooms. This file is the full, flattened documentation in plain text, intended for LLM ingestion. The rendered equivalent is at https://www.cojournalist.ai/docs.

Last updated: 2026-04-20. Canonical source: https://www.cojournalist.ai/llms-full.txt

---

## Table of contents

1. Introduction
2. Quickstart
3. Scouts
   3.1 Page Scout (`web`)
   3.2 Location Scout (`pulse`)
   3.3 Beat Scout (`pulse`)
   3.4 Social Scout (`social`)
   3.5 Civic Scout (`civic`)
4. Core concepts
   4.1 Information units
   4.2 Entities
   4.3 Deduplication
   4.4 Verification

   6.2 Structured extraction
   6.3 Export to a CMS
7. Reference
   7.1 Base URLs
   7.2 Authentication
   7.3 Endpoints
   7.4 Errors
   7.5 Credit costs
8. Self-hosting
9. Getting help

---

## 1. Introduction

coJournalist turns three tedious parts of reporting into background infrastructure:

- **Noticing** that a page changed
- **Understanding** whether a change matters
- **Remembering** what you already knew

You define a *scout* — a URL, a location, a social handle, a council domain — and coJournalist runs it on a schedule. It extracts structured information, deduplicates across sources, resolves entities, and drops the results into a knowledge graph that you own.

Every object is addressable by API. Your AI assistant can drive the workflow: pull unverified findings, group them by topic, flag dollar amounts and deadlines, draft a brief. You stay the verifier — every promotion stamps your user ID into `verified_by` with a timestamp for editorial audit.

**Who it's for.** Local and investigative reporters monitoring beats. Newsrooms tracking government and civic sources. Researchers following social accounts and niche publishers. Anyone who wants an AI assistant with editorial guardrails.

**What makes it different from scheduled LLM prompts.** Generic scheduled prompts re-surface the same article every time it moves. coJournalist uses Firecrawl `changeTracking` with per-scout baselines, atomic-fact extraction with pgvector dedup, PDF parsing for civic pipelines, post diffing for social, niche-source discovery in 12 languages, and explicit verification workflows. The output is a searchable knowledge graph, not a chat log.

---

## 2. Quickstart

Five minutes from zero to a running scout.

**Step 1 — Sign in.** Open https://www.cojournalist.ai and sign in with MuckRock OAuth. Free tier starts with 100 credits/month.

**Step 2 — Create your first scout.** Click **+ New scout** in the sidebar. Pick a type (start with Page Scout or Location Scout), name it, paste a URL or location, schedule it (daily / weekly / monthly). The first run establishes a baseline.

**Step 3 — Connect an AI assistant (optional).** Click **Agents** in the topbar. Pick MCP (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, Goose) or API (ChatGPT Actions, custom agents, scripts). Paste the snippet into your client's config. MCP uses full OAuth 2.1; you never paste tokens. REST uses a Bearer JWT you generate in the same modal.

**Step 4 — Verify findings.** Open a scout, skim new information units, click **Verify** on the ones that matter. Verified units are the ones your assistant can safely cite. Rejected units remain in the audit trail.

---

## 3. Scouts

Scouts are the unit of monitoring. Each has a type (determines the pipeline), a schedule (daily / weekly / monthly), and a project it belongs to. Per-run credit cost depends on type.

| Type | UI name | Purpose | Scope | Cost / run |
|------|---------|---------|-------|------------|
| `web` | Page Scout | Watch one URL for content changes | URL + optional topic filter | 1 |
| `pulse` | Location Scout | Local news for a geography, favouring niche sources | Location + optional criteria | 7 |
| `pulse` | Beat Scout | Topic news across reliable outlets | Criteria only | 7 |
| `social` | Social Scout | Monitor a social profile for new/removed posts | Platform + handle | 2 (IG/X/TikTok), 15 (Facebook) |
| `civic` | Civic Scout | Council agendas + promise extraction from PDFs | Council domain | 20 |

### 3.1 Page Scout (`web`)

Point it at any URL. Uses Firecrawl `changeTracking` with a per-scout tag, so each scout has its own baseline — you can track ten variants of the same page without interference. Only real content changes fire. An optional topic filter lets AI skip changes that don't match your criteria.

First-run behaviour is controlled by the "Import current page data" toggle:
- **Off (default)** — establishes baseline only; no unit is created on the first run
- **On** — extracts current content as the first unit in your knowledge base

### 3.2 Location Scout (`pulse`)

Give it a place (city, neighbourhood, district). Location Scout generates location-aware search queries in your preferred language (12 locales supported: en, de, fr, es, it, pt, nl, sv, no, da, fi, pl). It favours *niche* sources — local blogs, community publishers, regional sites — over national outlets. Output is atomic facts that passed similarity dedup against your prior runs.

### 3.3 Beat Scout (`pulse`)

Same underlying `pulse` pipeline as Location Scout, but sourced from *reliable* outlets (established publishers) and driven by criteria rather than location. Use it to watch a topic across geographies — housing supply decisions, state AG activity, FCC filings — where the where doesn't matter.

### 3.4 Social Scout (`social`)

Monitors Instagram, X, Facebook, LinkedIn, and TikTok profiles via Apify. Captures new posts and — importantly — **deletions** (valuable for monitoring politicians and PR firms). Image-aware criteria: filters can match on caption text, alt text, or image content. Facebook costs more (15 credits) because Meta makes it hard.

### 3.5 Civic Scout (`civic`)

Give it a council domain. Civic Scout discovers meeting pages, downloads agendas and minutes (often PDFs), and has Gemini extract **promises** — commitments, deadlines, dollar figures — with meeting-date context. Costs more (20 credits) because PDF parsing and promise extraction are expensive.

---

## 4. Core concepts

### 4.1 Information units

A *unit* is an atomic fact extracted from one or more sources.

> "Council approved $2.3M for SRP road paving with a Q4 2026 target."

That is one unit. Six articles reporting the decision become one unit with six `source_url` entries. Units carry:

- `occurred_at` — when the event happened
- `extracted_at` — when the scout found it
- `verified_by` — user ID of the editor who promoted it, or null
- `verified_at` — timestamp of verification, or null
- `tags` — free-form labels
- `entities` — references to resolved entity records
- `embedding` — Gemini multimodal vector, stored in pgvector for semantic search

### 4.2 Entities

People, organisations, locations, and documents are resolved into entities across units. "Salt River Pima Community" mentioned in four units resolves to one entity. Entities have their own knowledge page and cross-link to every unit that mentions them.

### 4.3 Deduplication

Dedup operates at four layers, so nothing gets surfaced twice:

- **Fact-level** — semantic similarity on extracted facts. Six rewrites of the same wire story become one unit.
- **Source-level** — each unit stores every source URL, so your assistant can cite the original and the follow-ups.
- **Entity-level** — entity resolution means "SRP", "Salt River Project", and "Salt River Pima" don't fracture into three histories.
- **Time-level** — queries can filter by `occurred_at` or `extracted_at`.

### 4.4 Verification

Units land in an unverified state. An editor reviews and calls `promote_unit` (MCP) / `POST /units/:id/verify` (REST) or the equivalent reject action. Every verification stamps the editor's user ID into `verified_by` with a timestamp.

AI assistants can draft using any unit but by convention cite only verified ones. The verification state is exposed on every API response so agents can be configured to hold the line.

### 4.5 Credits

Credits are the unit of cost. Every scout run decrements the credit balance on the project (individual users) or organisation (team plans).

- Free — 100 credits/month
- Pro — 1,000 credits/month
- Team — 5,000 credits/month, shared across the organisation

Monthly budget math: `(cost per run) × (runs per month)`. A daily Page Scout uses 30 credits/month. A weekly Civic Scout uses 80.

---

## 5. Integrations

Three surfaces; pick the one that matches your client. Most journalists use all three at different points.

### 5.1 MCP server

coJournalist ships an embedded MCP (Model Context Protocol) server with its own OAuth 2.1 authorization server. RFC 8414 metadata at `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`; RFC 7591 dynamic client registration at `/register`. Your MCP client handles the full flow — you never paste tokens.

**Endpoint:** `https://www.cojournalist.ai/mcp`

**Example config (`claude_desktop_config.json`):**

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cojournalist": {
      "url": "https://www.cojournalist.ai/mcp",
      "transport": "http"
    }
  }
}
```

**Tools exposed (non-exhaustive):**

| Tool | Does |
|------|------|
| `list_scouts` | List scouts in the current project |
| `get_scout` | Fetch a scout by ID with latest run status |
| `run_scout` | Trigger an on-demand run (counts against credits) |
| `list_units` | List information units, filterable by scout / verified / time |
| `search_units` | Semantic search across units |
| `promote_unit` / `reject_unit` | Verification actions; stamps your user ID |
| `list_entities` | People, orgs, locations, documents |
| `ingest_url` | Ingest an ad-hoc URL into the knowledge base |
| `export_project` | Export a project as markdown or JSON |

### 5.2 REST API

**Base URL:** `https://www.cojournalist.ai/functions/v1`

**Auth:** Bearer JWT in the `Authorization` header. Generate a token from **Agents → API** in the app.

**Examples:**

```bash
curl https://www.cojournalist.ai/functions/v1/scouts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $COJO_TOKEN"

curl "https://www.cojournalist.ai/functions/v1/units?verified=false&limit=20" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $COJO_TOKEN"
```

**Response envelope (lists):**

```json
{
  "items": [ ... ],
  "pagination": {
    "total": 123,
    "offset": 0,
    "limit": 20,
    "has_more": true
  }
}
```

**OpenAPI 3.1 spec:** `https://www.cojournalist.ai/functions/v1/openapi-spec`
**Interactive reference:** `https://www.cojournalist.ai/swagger`

### 5.3 CLI (`cojo`)

`cojo` is a Deno-based binary that speaks the same REST API. Useful for shell pipelines, nightly scripts, and piping Markdown exports.

```bash
# Install (macOS)
curl -L https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os/releases/latest/download/cojo-mac \
  -o /usr/local/bin/cojo
chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cojo

# Configure (generate a cj_... API key at https://www.cojournalist.ai → Agents → API → Create key)
cojo config set api_url=https://www.cojournalist.ai/api
cojo config set auth_token=<cj_... API key>

# Use
cojo projects list
cojo scouts add --name "Council agenda" --type web --url https://example.gov
cojo scouts run <id>
cojo units list --since 7d --verified
cojo units verify <id> --notes "Cross-checked with minutes"
cojo units search --query "zoning variance"
cojo ingest url https://example.com/article --project <id>
cojo export claude --project <id> --limit 50 | pbcopy
```

Build from source (Deno 2):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os.git
cd cojournalist
deno install -A -n cojo cli/cojo.ts
```

---

## 6. Cookbook

### 6.1 Daily triage with an AI assistant

**You (to Claude Desktop):**

> Pull all unverified units from my Phoenix Council and Oakland local scouts this week. Group by topic, flag anything mentioning dollar amounts or deadlines, and draft a 150-word brief I can read over coffee.

**Claude (via MCP):**

1. `list_scouts()` → finds Phoenix Council and Oakland local.
2. `list_units(scout_ids=[…], verified=false, since='7d')` → 9 units.
3. `search_units(query='$M OR deadline OR by 2026')` → narrows to 4.
4. Drafts a 150-word brief with source links.
5. Waits for your `promote_unit` / `reject_unit` decisions.

The assistant never publishes on its own. `promote_unit` is the editorial checkpoint.

### 6.2 Structured extraction

**You (to ChatGPT with REST Action):**

> Pull the last 30 agenda items from my cityof-oakland civic scout. For each, return title, meeting date, and a one-line summary. Give it to me as a markdown table.

Behind the scenes: `GET /units?scout_id=…&limit=30` → `/units/search` narrows to agenda-relevant ones → the assistant renders the table.

### 6.3 Export to a CMS

```bash
cojo export claude --project phoenix-council --limit 50 \
  | pandoc -f markdown -t html \
  | curl -X POST https://cms.example.com/api/drafts \
      -H "Authorization: Bearer $CMS_TOKEN" \
      -H "Content-Type: text/html" --data-binary @-
```

Swap `--format claude` for `json` or `markdown` depending on downstream needs.

---

## 7. Reference

### 7.1 Base URLs

| Surface | URL |
|---------|-----|
| App | https://www.cojournalist.ai |
| REST API | https://www.cojournalist.ai/functions/v1 |
| MCP server | https://www.cojournalist.ai/mcp |
| OpenAPI spec (JSON) | /functions/v1/openapi-spec |
| Swagger UI | /swagger |
| llms.txt | /llms.txt |
| llms-full.txt | /llms-full.txt |

### 7.2 Authentication

**REST / CLI** — Bearer JWT in the `Authorization` header. Generate from **Agents → API** in the app. Tokens are scoped to your account and rotated from the same modal.

**MCP** — OAuth 2.1 with PKCE. RFC 8414 metadata at `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`. RFC 7591 dynamic client registration at `/register`. Your MCP client handles the flow.

### 7.3 Endpoints (summary)

Full spec at /swagger. Highlights:

| Method | Path | What |
|--------|------|------|
| GET | /projects | List projects |
| POST | /projects | Create project |
| GET | /scouts | List scouts |
| POST | /scouts | Create scout |
| GET | /scouts/:id | Fetch scout |
| POST | /execute-scout | Trigger a run |
| GET | /units | List information units |
| POST | /units/search | Semantic search |
| POST | /units/:id/verify | Promote a unit |
| POST | /units/:id/reject | Reject a unit |
| GET | /entities | List entities |
| POST | /ingest | Ingest URL or text |
| POST | /export-claude | Export project as Claude-flavoured Markdown |

Query-string params on list endpoints: `limit`, `offset`, `since` (relative, e.g. `7d`), `verified`, `scout_id`, `project_id`, `tag`.

### 7.4 Errors

Every error response:

```json
{
  "error": "human-readable message",
  "code": "machine_code"
}
```

Common codes:

- `unauthorized` — missing or expired token
- `forbidden` — token valid, resource not yours
- `not_found` — no such resource
- `insufficient_credits` — run would exceed your plan
- `rate_limited` — too many requests; retry with exponential backoff
- `validation_error` — request body failed schema validation
- `method_not_allowed` — endpoint doesn't accept that verb

### 7.5 Credit costs

| Action | Credits |
|--------|---------|
| Page Scout run (`web`) | 1 |
| Location / Beat Scout run (`pulse`) | 7 |
| Social Scout — Instagram / X / TikTok | 2 |
| Social Scout — Facebook | 15 |
| Civic Scout run | 20 |
| Ad-hoc data extraction | varies by channel |

Monthly budget = cost per run × runs per month.

---

## 8. Self-hosting

coJournalist is source-available under the Sustainable Use License. Use it freely for your newsroom; don't resell it as a hosted service. Self-hosted deployments run on your own Supabase project with your Firecrawl, Gemini, Apify, and Resend keys. Same feature set as SaaS. No telemetry.

The GitHub repo (https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os) ships an automated setup flow — drop your AI coding agent into the repo, run the `setup` skill, and it provisions everything from a fresh Supabase project to the Edge Functions and frontend.

---

## 9. Getting help

- FAQ — https://www.cojournalist.ai/faq — licensing, self-hosting, editorial workflow
- Pricing — https://www.cojournalist.ai/pricing — plans, credits, team seats
- Issues — https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os/issues — bugs and feature requests
- In-app **Feedback** — routes to Linear, read by the maintainer
- GitHub Discussions — https://github.com/buriedsignals/cojournalist-os/discussions
